questions: When a queued send errors out, should the queue pause (await user action) or continue draining the remaining items? Default assumption in the plan: continue draining.
Should the queue persist if the user closes the chat panel (`isOpen=false`)? Default assumption: yes, since it's local React state and the component remains mounted; closing only hides UI.
Should attachments captured at queue time be visible in the queued-row card (thumbnails) or is a count badge (e.g. "📎 3") sufficient? Default: count badge to keep stacking compact.
When the agent session is cancelled mid-processing, should already-queued messages be discarded (current plan) or kept so the user can retry on a new session? Default: discard.

success_criteria.criterion: While the agent is processing a message, the input field accepts typing and the Send button can be pressed.
Submitting while busy adds a card to a queued-messages stack above the input rather than calling the send mutation.
Each queued card shows the message text, a delete control, and up/down reorder controls (with up disabled at the top, down disabled at the bottom).
When the active send finishes (mutation resolves AND `isProcessing` is false AND `optimisticMessage` cleared), the head of the queue is removed and auto-sent.
Each queued message sends with the attachments captured at queue time, not the current clip selection — verifiable by changing selection between submissions.
Cancelling the session and creating a new session both clear the queue.
No server/DB/hook signature changes — diff is confined to `AgentChat.tsx` (and tests).
Existing AgentChat tests still pass; new tests cover queueing, drain-on-completion, and reorder/delete.
`AgentChat.tsx` stays under ~900 LOC and the queue logic is contained, not scattered.
Manual smoke: queueing 3 messages with different clip selections processes them in order with correct attachments per message.

assumptions: Local client-side queue only — no persistence across reloads, no server-side queue.
The existing `useSendMessage` mutation rejects or no-ops if called while a turn is in flight, so we must wait for `isProcessing` to flip false before draining (not just for `mutateAsync` to resolve).
`optimisticMessage` clearing is a reliable signal that the previous send's user turn has been written to session state and we can safely send the next.
Cancel and New-session actions should drop pending queued items (a new session/cancel is a context reset).
An error during a queued send should surface via the existing error banner and not block subsequent queued items from attempting (matches minimal scope).
Voice-transcribed messages flow through the same `handleSend` path and therefore also queue when busy — desired.
Adding `ChevronUp`/`ChevronDown` icons from `lucide-react` is fine (already a dependency, used elsewhere in the file).
Tests use the existing testing setup (likely vitest based on the repo); patterns can be matched from `AgentChat.test.tsx`.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
